segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: OK, now I'd like to ...
segments.zh: 好了，现在我想……

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Hi Thomas, how are you enjoying the course so far?
segments.zh: 嗨，托马斯，到目前为止你觉得这门课怎么样？

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Yeah, I think it's good.
segments.zh: 嗯，我觉得挺好的。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Remind me – why did you decide to study sports science?
segments.zh: 提醒我一下——你为什么决定学体育科学？你上学的时候不是想当职业运动员吗？

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Didn't you want to be a professional athlete when you were at school?

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Yeah – that was my goal, and all my classmates assumed I would achieve it; they thought I was brilliant.
segments.zh: 是啊，那曾经是我的目标，我所有的同学都以为我能实现；他们觉得我很厉害。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: That must have been a nice feeling.
segments.zh: 那感觉一定很好。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Mm, I thought I could win anything.
segments.zh: 嗯，我以为我能赢任何比赛。没有人跑得比我快。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: There was no one who could run faster than me.

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Exactly – so what happened?
segments.zh: 没错——那后来发生了什么？你爸妈希望你更“学术”一点吗？

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Did your mum and dad want you to be more 'academic'?

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Not at all.
segments.zh: 不客气。不过，也许他们当时应该再逼我一把。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Perhaps they should have pushed me harder, though.

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: What do you mean?
segments.zh: 你这话是什么意思？

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: I think I should have practised more.
segments.zh: 我觉得我本应该多练习的。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: That makes you say that?
segments.zh: 是什么让你这么说的？

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Well, I went out to Kenya for a couple of weeks to train ...
segments.zh: 嗯，我去了肯尼亚待了几周进行训练……

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Really!
segments.zh: 真的吗！我都不知道。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: I didn't know that.

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: I was chosen to go there out of loads of kids and run with some of the top teenage athletes in the world.
segments.zh: 我是从很多孩子中被选去那里的，和世界上一些顶尖的青少年运动员一起跑步。而且……我当时非常平静。我只是一直在想自己多么幸运。这是多么好的机会啊！家乡的每个人都为我感到骄傲。但一旦开始比赛，我很快就意识到自己还不够好。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: And ...

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: I was so calm about it.

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: I just kept thinking how fortunate I was.

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: What a great chance this was!

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Everyone back home was so proud of me.

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: But once we started competing, I very quickly realised I wasn't good enough.

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: That must have been a huge shock.
segments.zh: 那一定是个巨大的打击。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: I thought 'this can't be happening'!
segments.zh: 我当时想‘这不可能’！我习惯了赢。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: I was used to winning.

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: I'm sorry to hear that.
segments.zh: 听到这个我很难过。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: It's OK.
segments.zh: 没事。我现在已经走出来了，而且我觉得上大学课程更好，这个课程涵盖了那么多体育相关领域。会很好的。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: I'm over it now and I think it's much better to do a university course and this one has such a variety of sports-related areas.

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: It's going to be good.

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Oh, I agree – I chose it because of that.
segments.zh: 哦，我同意——我就是因为这个才选的。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: So Jeanne – have you thought of any ideas for the discussion session next week on technology and sport?
segments.zh: 那么珍妮，关于下周的科技与体育讨论环节，你有什么想法吗？

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: We have to cover more than one sport, don't we?
segments.zh: 我们得涵盖不止一项运动，对吧？

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 对。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: You know – we always think technology is about the future, but we could gather some ideas about past developments in sport.
segments.zh: 你知道——我们总以为科技是关于未来的，但我们可以收集一些关于体育过去发展的想法。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Look at early types of equipment perhaps?
segments.zh: 也许看看早期的设备类型？

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Uh, I remember reading something about table tennis bats once – how they ended up being covered with pimpled rubber.
segments.zh: 呃，我记得以前读过一些关于乒乓球拍的东西——它们最后是怎么被覆盖上颗粒橡胶的。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Cos they were just wooden at first, I'd imagine.
segments.zh: 因为我想它们一开始只是木制的。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 是的。大约在20世纪20年代，一家工厂大量生产橡胶卷，用于像马具之类的东西。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: In about the 1920s, a factory was making rolls of the rubber in bulk for something like horse harnesses.

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Really!
segments.zh: 真的吗！

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Yeah – and someone realised that it'd make a perfect covering for the wooden bats.
segments.zh: 是的——然后有人意识到这可以完美地覆盖木制球拍。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: So what about cricket – that's had a few innovative changes.
segments.zh: 那板球呢？它有一些创新的变化。也许他们腿上戴的护垫？

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Maybe the pads they wear on their legs?

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: I don't think they've changed much but, I'm just looking on the internet ... and it says that when the first cricket helmet came in, in 1978, the Australian batsman who first wore it was booed and jeered by people watching because it was so ugly!
segments.zh: 我觉得它们变化不大，但我正在网上查……上面说当第一个板球头盔在1978年问世时，第一个戴它的澳大利亚击球手被观众喝倒彩和嘲笑，因为它太丑了！

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Wow, players have to protect themselves from getting hurt!
segments.zh: 哇，球员们得保护自己不受伤！我是说现在每个人都戴头盔了。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: I mean everyone wears one now.

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Mm, unlike the cycle helmet.
segments.zh: 嗯，不像自行车头盔。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Well, unless you're a professional, but you're right, many ordinary bikers don't wear a helmet.
segments.zh: 嗯，除非你是职业选手，但你说得对，很多普通骑车的人不戴头盔。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Hey, look at these pictures of original helmet designs.
segments.zh: 嘿，看看这些原始头盔设计的图片。这个看起来像个倒扣的碗！

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: This one looks like an upside-down bowl!

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Yet, the woman's laughing – she's so proud to be wearing it!
segments.zh: 然而，那女人在笑——她戴着它很自豪！

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: It says serious cyclists ended up with wet hair from all the hard exercise.
segments.zh: 上面说认真的自行车手因为剧烈运动最后头发都湿了。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: I guess that's why they have large air vents in them now so that the skin can breathe more easily.
segments.zh: 我猜这就是为什么现在头盔上有大的通风口，这样皮肤可以更容易呼吸。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: OK, so we've done helmets.
segments.zh: 好了，头盔说完了。那高尔夫球或者更准确地说高尔夫球杆呢——它们变化很大。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: What about golf balls or better still golf clubs – they've changed a lot.

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Yeah – I remember my great grandfather telling me that because a club was made entirely of wood, it would easily break and players had to get another.
segments.zh: 是啊，我记得我曾祖父跟我说过，因为球杆是全木制的，很容易断，球员就得换一根。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: There's no wood at all in them now, is there?
segments.zh: 现在它们里面一点木头都没有了，是吧？

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: No – they're much more powerful.
segments.zh: 没有了，它们现在威力大多了。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: The same must be true of hockey sticks.
segments.zh: 曲棍球杆肯定也一样吧。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: I don't think so because players still use wooden sticks today.
segments.zh: 我不这么认为，因为现在球员还在用木制球杆。不过这里确实提到，这项运动刚开始时，你得自己制作球杆。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: What it does say here, though, is that when the game started you had to produce a stick yourself.

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: I guess they just weren't being manufactured.
segments.zh: 我猜那时候还没有批量生产。那么，再问一个吧。足球呢？

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: So, one more perhaps.

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: What about football?

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Well, I know the first balls were made of animal skin.
segments.zh: 嗯，我知道最早的足球是用动物皮做的。

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Yeah, they covered them with pieces of leather that were stitched together, but ... the balls let in water when it rained.
segments.zh: 是啊，他们用缝在一起的皮革片包住球，但是……下雨的时候球会进水。

segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: Oh, that would have made them much heavier.
segments.zh: 哦，那会让它们重得多。

items.prompt: Which TWO points do Thomas and Jeanne make about Thomas's sporting activities at school?

pairs.note: 题干中的'sporting activities'被替换为'goal'，指代体育目标。
原文与题干完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的'classmates assumed'直接定位到原文，再结合'my goal'对应'sporting activities'，找到答案C。

items.prompt: Which TWO feelings did Thomas experience when he was in Kenya?

pairs.note: 题干问感受，原文用'认为'表达了一种心理状态，属于同义替换。
题干中的Thomas在原文中用'I'指代，是代词替换。
题干问感受，原文用'认为'表达了一种心理状态，属于同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'my classmates assumed I would achieve it; they thought I was brilliant'，其中'assumed'和'thought'对应题干中的'feelings'，表示同学对Thomas的看法，从而定位到Thomas的感受。

pairs.note: 题干中的'乒乓球拍'对应原文中的'橡胶'，因为乒乓球拍通常由橡胶制成。

paraphrase.explain: 通过'rubber'定位到原文中关于橡胶生产的描述，从而找到答案。

pairs.note: 原文中直接出现，但加了'first'，意思一致。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是'cricket helmet'，定位句提到'the first cricket helmet'，直接对应，可定位到答案F。

pairs.note: 题干中的'cycle helmet'与原文中的'serious cyclists'是同义替换，都指骑自行车的人。

paraphrase.explain: 题干关键词'cycle helmet'在原文中被替换为'serious cyclists'，通过同义替换定位到答案。

pairs.note: 题干中的golf club在原文中简称为club，是同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的golf club在原文中直接对应club，通过这个同义替换定位到答案D。

pairs.note: 题干说冰球杆，原文说木制球杆，是同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是冰球杆，原文提到球员仍使用木制球杆，所以定位到此处。
